error_signature: first column not resolving in CSV
title: First column not resolving in CSV input
symptom: When reading a CSV file with a semicolon delimiter, the first column fails to resolve correctly, while subsequent columns work as expected.
applies_when: First column is in UTF-8-BOM format
not_this_if: CSV file is in UTF-8 format
root_cause: The issue arises from the CSV file being encoded in UTF-8-BOM format, which can cause parsing issues specifically with the first column. This encoding includes a byte order mark that may interfere with how the CSV node interprets the data.
fix_steps: Open the CSV file in a text editor
Change the encoding from UTF-8-BOM to UTF-8
Update the CSV node settings in n8n to reflect the new encoding
agent_instruction: Convert the CSV file encoding from UTF-8-BOM to UTF-8 and update the node settings.
